_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that different text types across all our subjects have characteristic structures and language features that suit their purpose.

_source.w: We are learning that different text types across all our subjects have characteristic structures and language features that suit their purpose.
y: Understanding text structures helps us comprehend and create texts more efficiently.
Different subjects require different text types, and knowing their features helps us succeed across the curriculum.
This knowledge makes us more strategic readers and writers.
eg: We identify the characteristic stages of a narrative — orientation, complication, resolution.
We compare how a historical recount is structured versus a scientific explanation.
We find the language features that make a persuasive text convincing — like emotive words and rhetorical questions.
We create a reference chart showing the key features of text types we use regularly.
We analyse a text and identify its type based on its structure and language.
We choose the right text structure when planning a piece of writing for a specific subject.
